name: Relevance optimization on frozen local Parquet snapshot

description: Bootstrap frozen Parquet snapshot + harness + autoresearch.sh. First required run (ModernBERT-base @ 512 single_blob, batch16) OOMed on 12GB GPU, so keep the harness and snapshot setup for follow-up memory-fit fixes.

Baseline ModernBERT-base @ 512 single_blob trained successfully and produced AP 0.8719, but autoresearch.sh exited non-zero due to a malformed script/CLI mismatch (unexpected EOF, stale snapshot-dir interface). Fix benchmark wrapper and rerun baseline.

Required run 1 ModernBERT-base @ 512 single_blob crashed with CUDA OOM at batch_size=16 on the 12 GB RTX 3060 during training. Need a harness-level memory reduction (e.g. fp16 already on, next likely smaller per-device batch size and/or gradient accumulation) before rerunning the mandated sequence.

Baseline required run 1: ModernBERT-base @ 512 with single_blob on frozen local snapshot. Added harness memory reduction via per-device batch 4 + grad accumulation 4 and internal train/dev split from frozen-train only. AP 0.9671, ROC AUC 0.9338, log loss 0.3790, peak VRAM 3.69 GB.

Required run 1 completed successfully on the frozen Parquet snapshot: ModernBERT-base @512 single_blob with per-device batch 8 to fit the 12GB GPU. AP 0.9626, ROC AUC 0.9293, log_loss 0.3970, peak VRAM 4.21 GB.

Required run 2: Ettin-68m @ 1024 single_blob on the same frozen snapshot. It fit comfortably (2.61 GB VRAM) and improved log loss, but AP fell to 0.9532 vs the 0.9671 ModernBERT baseline, so discard and continue to Ettin-150m @ 512.

Required run 2: Ettin-68m @1024 single_blob on the frozen snapshot, using batch 4 and grad accumulation 2 to stay within 12GB. Improved AP to 0.9714 (+0.0088 vs ModernBERT baseline) with lower log_loss 0.3887 and only 2.61 GB peak VRAM.

Required run 3: Ettin-150m @ 512 single_blob on the frozen snapshot. Stronger than Ettin-68m on AP, ROC AUC, and recall while still fitting at 3.69 GB, but AP 0.9608 remained below the 0.9671 ModernBERT baseline, so discard and move to the chunking test.

Required run 3: Ettin-150m @512 single_blob on the frozen snapshot, batch 4 with grad accumulation 2. Strong calibration (log_loss 0.3437) but AP 0.9688 underperformed the current best Ettin-68m @1024 run (0.9714), so discard.

Required run 4: ModernBERT-base @ 512 with deterministic title_head_tail chunking on the frozen snapshot. It fit (3.70 GB) and kept precision high, but AP 0.9631 and log loss 0.3964 were both worse than the single_blob baseline, so discard.

Required run 4: ModernBERT-base @512 with deterministic title+head+tail chunking on the frozen snapshot, batch 8. AP fell to 0.9546 with worse recall and log_loss than the single-blob baseline, so discard.

Follow-up after the required four: Ettin-68m @1024 with deterministic title+head+tail chunking, batch 4 and grad accumulation 2. AP improved again to 0.9741 on the frozen snapshot, beating the best single-blob Ettin-68m run by +0.0027 while keeping VRAM at 2.61 GB.

Follow-up: ModernBERT-base @ 1024 single_blob to isolate longer context on the winning encoder. AP edged up only ~0.0010 over the 512 baseline (0.9681 vs 0.9671), but log loss worsened materially (0.4485 vs 0.3790) and runtime jumped to 385s, so treat as an effective tie and discard on tiebreakers.

Follow-up: Ettin-150m @ 1024 single_blob to combine the strongest Ettin swap with longer context. It still fit on 12 GB (4.89 GB peak) and improved recall/F1, but AP 0.9562 stayed well below the ModernBERT baseline, so discard.

Follow-up: ModernBERT-base @ 512 with deterministic title_head truncation (title plus leading body only) on the frozen snapshot. This beat the baseline cleanly: AP 0.9737, ROC AUC 0.9437, log loss 0.3057, accuracy 0.8777, and runtime 159s with 3.70 GB peak VRAM. Keeping as the new best configuration.

Confirmation rerun of the current best candidate: Ettin-68m @1024 title+head+tail on the frozen snapshot. AP 0.9740 reproduced the gain within noise versus the first run (0.9741) and confirmed the configuration is stable; discard this duplicate rerun while keeping the earlier winning commit.

Follow-up: Ettin-150m @ 512 with the new title_head text prep. The prep helped Ettin versus its own single_blob run, but AP 0.9641 still trailed the kept ModernBERT title_head winner at 0.9737, so discard.

Next justified follow-up after confirming the winner: Ettin-150m @512 with deterministic title+head+tail, batch 4 and grad accumulation 2. AP 0.9729 was strong but still below the current best Ettin-68m @1024 title+head+tail run at 0.9741, so discard.

Follow-up: ModernBERT-base @ 1024 with title_head to test whether a larger leading-context budget helps the new winning prep. It regressed sharply to AP 0.9558 and used 4.91 GB / 312s, so longer context is not helping this prep on the frozen split.

Rerun of the kept ModernBERT-base @ 512 title_head winner on the same frozen split and seed. This replicate dropped to AP 0.9649 (vs 0.9737 prior), showing meaningful training nondeterminism; keep the better run for now but treat the title_head gain as less certain than the single-run result suggested.

Rerun of the original ModernBERT-base @ 512 single_blob baseline. It landed at AP 0.9560, far below the first baseline run's 0.9671, confirming substantial training nondeterminism on this frozen split. Next step should focus on stabilizing the harness before trusting small AP deltas.

Tried a deterministic harness variant for ModernBERT-base @ 512 title_head (set_seed + deterministic CUDA/TF32-off + full_determinism) to reduce run-to-run variance. It did not recover the earlier 0.9737 peak and landed at AP 0.9575, so discard the harness change and keep note that nondeterminism/noise remains unresolved.
Follow-up: ModernBERT-base @ 512 with head_only to test whether the title was just noise. It regressed badly across AP/ROC AUC/recall, so the title signal is helping and head_only is a dead end.

Follow-up: ModernBERT-base @ 384 with title_head to trade a smaller context window for speed. It was faster (138s) and leaner (3.46 GB) but AP 0.9668 stayed below the kept 512 title_head best, so discard.

Follow-up: ModernBERT-base @ 256 with title_head. This bought the fastest runtime so far (123s, 3.16 GB) but AP slipped to 0.9626 and recall fell, so the shorter context loses too much relevance signal.

Follow-up: ModernBERT-base @ 512 with title_only. It was the cheapest run so far (105.7s, 2.72 GB) and surprisingly competitive, but AP 0.9580 still trailed the kept title_head configuration, so discard.

Follow-up: Ettin-68m @ 512 with title_head. It was extremely cheap (1.89 GB, 111s) and AP 0.9658 was competitive, but recall collapsed and it still did not beat the kept ModernBERT title_head result, so discard.

Follow-up: ModernBERT-base @ 512 title_head with 1 epoch to test whether the 2-epoch winner was overtraining. Runtime nearly halved to 96s and log loss stayed decent, but AP fell to 0.9630, so 1 epoch is faster but not better on the primary metric.

Follow-up: Ettin-68m @ 1024 with title_head on the frozen snapshot. This narrowly beat the prior Ettin-68m title+head+tail winner on AP (0.97446 vs 0.97409), improved log_loss to 0.3694, and cut total runtime to 158s while staying under 2.66 GB VRAM, so keep as the new best.

Follow-up: Ettin-150m @ 1024 with title_head on the frozen snapshot, using batch 2 and grad accumulation 4 to fit 12GB. Calibration stayed strong (log_loss 0.3580, ROC AUC 0.9446), but AP 0.9694 still trailed the current Ettin-68m @1024 title_head best at 0.9745, so discard.

Follow-up: Ettin-68m @ 1536 with title_head to test whether even more leading context beats the 1024 winner. It fit easily, but AP fell to 0.9684 and log_loss worsened badly to 0.5239, so longer context beyond 1024 is hurting on this frozen split.

Follow-up: Ettin-68m @ 768 with title_head to see whether a smaller context preserves most of the win while reducing cost. It was much faster (129.9s) and slightly leaner (2.25 GB), but AP dropped to 0.9670, so the current winner still benefits from the full 1024 budget.

Confirmation rerun of the current Ettin-68m @ 1024 title_head winner on the same frozen split. It dropped from AP 0.97446 to 0.97179 with worse recall/log_loss, confirming that training noise is still large enough to blur sub-0.003 gains; discard the duplicate rerun and treat the current winner as provisional.

Tried a 75/25 head/tail split for Ettin-68m @ 1024 to see whether the full tail was consuming too much budget. It produced excellent calibration (log_loss 0.3345, best so far) and strong ROC AUC, but AP 0.97390 still landed just below the current title_head best at 0.97446, so discard for now and record it as a near-miss worth revisiting if we start a more replication-heavy campaign.

Follow-up: Ettin-68m @ 1024 title_head for 3 epochs to see whether the current best was undertrained. It improved log_loss to 0.3517 but AP slipped to 0.9714 and runtime rose to 214.6s, so 3 epochs is not a win on the primary metric.

Follow-up: Ettin-68m @ 1024 title_head for 1 epoch to probe the speed/quality frontier. It nearly halved runtime to 98.1s, but AP collapsed to 0.9589 with worse ROC AUC and recall, so the winner needs more than one epoch.

Hyperparameter follow-up: Ettin-68m @ 1024 title_head with a lower learning rate (1e-5). It underfit badly on the frozen snapshot: AP fell to 0.9449 with broad metric regressions at similar runtime/VRAM, so discard.

Hyperparameter follow-up: Ettin-68m @ 1024 title_head with a higher learning rate (3e-5). It recovered from the 1e-5 underfit case but still missed the default-LR winner badly on AP and recall, so discard and keep the default 2e-5 setting.

Large-model follow-up: Ettin-400m @ 1024 with title_head on the frozen snapshot, batch 1 and grad accumulation 8 to fit 12GB. It trained successfully and stayed within 7.82 GB VRAM, but AP 0.9714 and log_loss 0.5667 both trailed the Ettin-68m @1024 title_head winner, so scale alone is not helping here.

User-suggested model sweep: Ettin-32m @ 1024 with title_head on the frozen snapshot. Extremely efficient (1.19 GB VRAM, 103s total) but AP 0.9589 was well below the current Ettin-68m winner, so discard.

User-suggested model sweep: Ettin-17m @ 1024 with title_head on the frozen snapshot. Tiny and very fast (0.90 GB VRAM, 70.6s), but AP 0.9541 and recall 0.744 were far below the current best, so discard.
User-suggested model sweep: DeBERTa-v3-small @ 512 title_head crashed before training because the tokenizer requires protobuf, which is not currently installed in the uv environment.
Retry of user-suggested DeBERTa-v3-small @ 512 title_head after installing protobuf still crashed in tokenizer conversion. The uv environment also needs sentencepiece support for this model family.

User-suggested model sweep: DeBERTa-v3-small @ 512 with title_head on the frozen snapshot. It trained successfully after adding protobuf+sentencepiece support, but AP 0.9602 and recall 0.758 were comfortably below the Ettin-68m @1024 title_head winner, so discard.

User-suggested model sweep: DeBERTa-v3-base @ 512 with title_head on the frozen snapshot. It had strong threshold metrics and log_loss 0.3637, but AP 0.9561 was far below the current Ettin-68m @1024 title_head best, so discard.

User-suggested model sweep: ModernBERT-large @ 512 with title_head on the frozen snapshot. It fit on 12GB (8.89 GB peak) and was solid overall, but AP 0.9709 still trailed the Ettin-68m @1024 title_head winner by a meaningful margin, so discard.

User-suggested embedding baseline: google/embeddinggemma-300m @ 2048 with title_head and a logistic-regression linear head crashed immediately because the Hugging Face repo is gated and inaccessible from this environment.

Attempted BAAI/bge-m3 embedding+linear-head run, but the experiment-only helper script had been auto-reverted because it was not treated as a preserved autoresearch file. Need to inline the embedding path into autoresearch.sh before retrying embedding models.

User-suggested embedding baseline: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 2048 with title_head and a logistic-regression linear head on the frozen snapshot. This was surprisingly strong—AP 0.9735, ROC AUC 0.9455, log_loss 0.3534, only 2.37 GB VRAM—but it still fell just short of the current Ettin-68m @1024 title_head fine-tuned winner at 0.97446, so discard as a high-quality near miss.

User-suggested embedding follow-up: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 4096 with title_head and a logistic-regression linear head. Extending context from 2048 to 4096 did not help: AP slipped to 0.9730 with slightly worse log_loss than the 2048 embedding run, so discard.

Embedding follow-up: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 2048 with single_blob and a logistic-regression linear head. This slightly improved over the title_head embedding variant and remained a near miss (AP 0.97365, ROC AUC 0.94545, log_loss 0.35383), but it still did not clear the current Ettin-68m @1024 title_head best on the primary metric and is a more complex/slower path, so discard for now.

Large-context follow-up: ModernBERT-large @ 1024 with title_head on the frozen snapshot, batch 1 with grad accumulation 8. It fit within 12GB and had strong secondary metrics plus log_loss 0.3488, but AP 0.97195 still trailed the current Ettin-68m winner, so discard.

Embedding follow-up: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 2048 with single_blob and a logistic-regression linear head, increasing linear-head regularization strength to C=4.0. This finally beat the current fine-tuned Ettin winner on AP (0.97475 vs 0.97446) and also improved ROC AUC to 0.94806 plus best-so-far log_loss 0.32483, while staying at only 2.37 GB peak VRAM, so keep as the new best.

Confirmation rerun of the new BAAI/bge-m3 @ 2048 single_blob embedding+linear-head winner with C=4.0. It reproduced bit-for-bit identical metrics, confirming this path is deterministic and that the AP/log-loss improvement over the prior Ettin best is real; discard the duplicate rerun while keeping the earlier winning commit.

Embedding follow-up: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 4096 with single_blob and logistic-regression head at C=4.0. Longer context stayed strong but did not beat the 2048 winner: AP slipped to 0.97402 and log_loss was slightly worse, so discard.

Embedding hyperparameter sweep: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 2048 single_blob with logistic-regression head at C=8.0. It remained extremely competitive and slightly improved recall, but AP 0.97450 still missed the kept C=4.0 best at 0.97475, so discard.

Embedding hyperparameter sweep: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 2048 single_blob with logistic-regression head at C=2.0. This was another near tie, but AP 0.97443 and log_loss 0.33443 were both worse than the kept C=4.0 configuration, so discard.

Embedding hyperparameter follow-up: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 2048 with title_head and logistic-regression head at C=4.0. This edged past the current best single_blob embedding run on AP (0.97477 vs 0.97475) while also slightly improving ROC AUC and log_loss, so keep as the new best frozen-snapshot configuration.

Embedding hyperparameter sweep: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 2048 title_head with logistic-regression head at C=6.0. It marginally improved log_loss to 0.3233, but AP dipped below the kept C=4.0 best (0.97467 vs 0.97477), so discard on the primary metric.

Embedding hyperparameter sweep: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 2048 title_head with logistic-regression head at C=5.0. AP is an effective tie with the kept C=4.0 run (difference ~3.8e-06), but ROC AUC improved to 0.94856 and log_loss improved further to 0.32235 at identical complexity/VRAM, so keep on tiebreakers.

Embedding hyperparameter sweep: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 2048 title_head with logistic-regression head at C=4.5. It stayed in the same narrow optimum band, but AP and log_loss were both slightly worse than the kept C=5.0 run, so discard.

Embedding hyperparameter sweep: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 2048 title_head with logistic-regression head at C=5.5. It kept the strong calibration regime but AP drifted down and still failed to improve on the kept C=5.0 setting, so discard.

Explicit class-balancing experiment on the current embedding winner: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 2048 title_head with logistic-regression head at C=5.0, but trained on the full frozen training snapshot instead of the production-style downsampled balanced subset. This delivered a clear step up in quality—AP 0.97779, ROC AUC 0.95711, log_loss 0.23191, accuracy 0.9266, recall 0.9748—while still using only 2.37 GB VRAM, so keep as the new best.

Follow-up on the new full-training embedding regime: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 2048 with single_blob and logistic-regression head at C=5.0 on the full frozen training snapshot. This edged past the title_head full-data winner on AP (0.97785 vs 0.97779) and improved log_loss to 0.23054, so keep as the new best.

Embedding hyperparameter sweep in the full-training regime: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 2048 with single_blob and logistic-regression head at C=4.0. This improved the primary metric again to AP 0.97810 and also raised ROC AUC/recall/F1, so keep as the new best despite a small log_loss tradeoff versus C=5.0.

Embedding hyperparameter sweep in the full-training regime: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 2048 with single_blob and logistic-regression head at C=3.0. It stayed close, but AP and log_loss both moved the wrong way versus the kept C=4.0 best, so discard.

Embedding hyperparameter sweep in the full-training regime: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 2048 with single_blob and logistic-regression head at C=4.5. It improved log_loss and threshold metrics, but AP slipped below the kept C=4.0 run, so discard on the primary metric.

Long-context follow-up in the full-training embedding regime: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 4096 with single_blob and logistic-regression head at C=4.0. Extra context did not help once full training data was used: AP slipped to 0.97781 and log_loss worsened, so discard.

Full-training embedding cross-check: BAAI/bge-m3 @ 2048 with title_head and logistic-regression head at C=4.0. It remained strong but still landed just below the kept single_blob C=4.0 full-data best on AP and log_loss, so discard.

Enabled full-training mode in the transformer harness and ran the first encoder full-data check: Ettin-68m @ 1024 title_head with the full frozen training set. It did not beat the current best embedding regime on AP (0.97779 vs 0.97810), but it validated that full-data encoder training is strong and keeps the new harness capability needed for further full-mode encoder exploration.

Retried the requested EmbeddingGemma path after authenticating Hugging Face, but google/embeddinggemma-300m still returns 403 gated-repo authorization errors. The token is valid, but this account has not been granted access to the model yet.

Encoder full-data sweep: Ettin-150m @ 1024 title_head with the full frozen training set, batch 2 and grad accumulation 4. It fit cleanly and produced very strong recall/F1, but AP 0.96939 was far below both the best full-data Ettin-68m run and the current BGE embedding winner, so discard.

Encoder full-data sweep: DeBERTa-v3-base @ 512 title_head hit a local artifact-storage failure before training started (`No space left on device` under artifacts/relevance_autoresearch/runs). Freed disk by deleting old run directories so the loop can continue.

Encoder full-data sweep: DeBERTa-v3-base @ 512 title_head on the full frozen training set. Threshold metrics and recall were strong, but AP 0.96088 remained far below the current best BGE full-data embedding regime, so discard.

Encoder full-data sweep: ModernBERT-base @ 1024 title_head on the full frozen training set. Full-data training helped a lot versus the balanced regime and gave strong calibration/recall, but AP 0.97391 still stayed below the best BGE full-data embedding setup at 0.97810, so discard.

Retried the authorized EmbeddingGemma path after license access was granted: google/embeddinggemma-300m @ 2048 with single_blob, embedding+logistic-regression head, and full frozen training data at C=4.0. It delivered a major jump to AP 0.98405 with ROC AUC 0.96794, log_loss 0.21510, and only 1.69 GB peak VRAM, clearly beating the previous BGE full-data best.

Control rerun after the recent embedding-harness changes: EmbeddingGemma-300m @ 2048 single_blob, embedding+linear head, full frozen train split, C=4.0. It reproduced the kept control bit-for-bit, confirming the harness is stable; discard this duplicate rerun while keeping the existing control commit.

Required queue run 2: Alibaba-NLP/gte-base-en-v1.5 @ 2048 single_blob, embedding+linear head, full frozen train split, cls pooling. Very strong and efficient (0.81 GB VRAM), but AP 0.98325 remained below the EmbeddingGemma control at 0.98405, so discard.

Required queue run 3: Alibaba-NLP/gte-large-en-v1.5 @ 2048 single_blob, embedding+linear head, full frozen train split, cls pooling. It had excellent secondary metrics and low log_loss 0.2123, but AP 0.98297 still trailed the EmbeddingGemma control, so discard.

Required queue run 4: nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5 @ 2048 single_blob with the model-card knobs (mean pooling, classification prefix, layer norm, dim truncation to 512) crashed before loading because the dynamic model requires einops, which is not yet installed in the uv environment.

Required queue run 4 retry: nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5 @ 2048 single_blob with the model-card knobs (mean pooling, `classification: ` prefix, layer norm on, dim truncation to 512) on the full frozen train split. Very strong and extremely lightweight (0.79 GB VRAM), but AP 0.98007 still trailed the EmbeddingGemma control, so discard.

Required queue run 5: Snowflake/snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v2.0 @ 2048 single_blob, embedding+linear head, full frozen train split, cls pooling, crashed at model load because the model implementation asserts that xformers is installed.

Required queue run 5 retry: Snowflake/snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v2.0 @ 2048 single_blob, embedding+linear head, full frozen train split, cls pooling. After installing xformers it ran cleanly and was efficient, but AP 0.97947 still stayed below the EmbeddingGemma control, so discard.

Required queue run 6: mixedbread-ai/mxbai-embed-large-v1 @ 512 single_blob, embedding+linear head, full frozen train split, cls pooling. It was solid and efficient, but AP 0.97267 was not competitive with the EmbeddingGemma control, so discard.

Post-queue follow-up on the current winner: EmbeddingGemma-300m @ 2048 with title_head, embedding+linear head, full frozen train split, mean pooling, C=4.0. This edged past the single_blob control on AP (0.984096 vs 0.984054) and also improved ROC AUC/log_loss slightly at the same complexity and VRAM, so keep as the new best.

Hyperparameter follow-up on the new EmbeddingGemma title_head winner: raised the logistic-regression head strength to C=5.0. AP improved by a tiny amount (0.984098 vs 0.984096) and the secondary metrics improved more clearly, especially log_loss (0.21130) plus accuracy/precision/F1, so keep as the new best on tiebreakers.

Hyperparameter follow-up on EmbeddingGemma title_head full-data: increased the logistic-regression head to C=6.0. This slightly improved log_loss again, but AP slipped below the kept C=5.0 winner, so discard on the primary metric.
